english: tect, to shelter, e.g. lus (lus), the body, but also: to keep unpolluted, pure, chaste; bdag sruṅ-ba (bdag srung ba) to guard one's self, in a special sense: to live as a bdag-sruṅ (bdag srung), as a hermit Dzl.; to preserve, bdag gnód-pa tams-cád-las sruṅs śig (bdag gnod pa thams cad las srungs shig) may I be preserved from every harm! Do.; with la: bdág-la sruṅ-du gsol (bdag la srung du gsol) I pray to preserve, to protect me Do. - 2. to beware of, to guard against, lus daṅ ṅág-gi nyés-pa (lus dang ngag gi nyes pa) Dzl., = lus daṅ ṅag sruṅ-ba (lus dang ngag srung ba) (v. above no. 1) Dzl. - 3. to keep, to observe faithfully, a promise, laws; bká - sruṅ - mkán (bka' srung mkhan) obedient, faithful, trustworthy. - 4. to hinder, forbid, prohibit, rígs-kyis (rigs kyis), bdág-pos (bdag pos), ćós-kyis sruṅ (chos kyis srung) it is forbidden, it is prohibited, by the degree of kindred, by the husband, by religion in general Thgy.; to prevent, to be a preservative or preventive S.g. - 5. to wait, = sriṅ-ba (sring ba), e.g. *źag nyi* (*zhag nyi*) for two days W. II. sbst. 1. the keeping, guarding, the heed, guard. - 2. the person or the thing keeping, guarding, esp. amulet, preventive, preservative, btágs-pa (btags pa) to suspend (an amulet, to the neck or other part of the body). Comp. and deriv. sruṅ-skúd (srung skud), -_kór (-'khor) or -mdúd (-mdud) an amulet consisting of threads. - sruṅ-mkán (srung mkhan) keeper, guardian, watchman, *tsán-la sruṅ - kán* (*mtshan la srung mkhan*) W. (night-) watchman; sruṅ-pa (srung pa) B. = sruṅ-mkán (srung mkhan), bzá-śiṅ-ra-ba sruṅ-pa (bza' shing ra ba srung pa) keeper of a fruit-garden Dzl.; sruṅ-po (srung po) Cs. = sruṅ-mkán (srung mkhan); sruṅ-ma (srung ma) B. id., dmyal-bai sruṅ-ma (dmyal ba'i srung ma) guardian of the infernal regions frq. Dzl.; ćós-skyoṅ-bai sruṅ-ma tams-cád (chos skyong ba'i srung ma thams cad) all the tutelar gods of religion Mil.; collectively: body of watchmen, lhá-rnams-kyi sruṅ-ma dáṅ-po (lha rnams kyi srung ma dang po) the first corps of watchmen of the gods, the Nāga; rgyál-poi sruṅ-mai mi (rgyal po'i srung ma'i mi) the men of the king's body-guard Stg. -sruṅ-sems (-srung sems) the taking heed, being cautious.

calm, soft, mild, and: mildness, gentleness, meekness; srún-po (srun po) adj. = srún-pa (srun pa), esp. of horses: quiet, tame; śin-tu mi-bsrún-źiṅ (shin tu mi bsrun zhing) very malicious, malignant, of demons Mil.

pf. imp. (b)srubs ((b)srubs), fut. bsrub (bsrub), 1. to stir, stir up, stir about, źo srúb-pa (zho srub pa) to churn, to make butter. - 2. to rummage, to rake up, to stir, to turn over. - 3. to rub, two pieces of wood against each other Wdn. - *srub-śiṅ* (*srub shing*) C. 1. twirling-stick. 2. mischiefmaker, disturber of the peace.

1. a cleft, slit, gap, fissure, brag-srúbs (brag srubs) chasm or cleft in a rock, smaller than sér-ka (ser ka) Mil.; intermediate space, interval, interstice; rent in a dress; disunion, separation; wound Lt.; srubs ₒbye (srubs 'bye) Lt., srubs ₒtor (srubs 'thor) Sch. a severing, a wound has been made; srubs gtór-ba (srubs gtor ba) to rend asunder, to tear Sch. *tsem-śrúb dól-ce* (*tshem srub 'drol ce*) W. to rip, to cut open a seam. - 2. seam? - 3. W. col. for srus (srus).
resp. for meat, flesh of animals used as food, srum-kóg (srum kog) an animal slaughtered and cut up, for a person of quality.
1. evil demon, malignant spirit Mil.; lús-srul-po (lus srul po) Lex. sorcerer. - 2. putrid, rotten Cs.

pf. and fut. bsrul (bsrul), I. to be corrupted, decomposed, of the humours of the body Wdn. II. W. *śrúl-ce* (*srul ce*), = śrúg-ce* (*srug ce*) 1. to stir, *túg-pa* (*thug pa*) the soup, to mix and stir, *čú-la p'e* (*chu la phye*) flour with water. - 2. to shove, to move, to and fro, *pág-te śrúl-ce* (*pag te srul ce*) to plane, *čad-sóg śrul-ce* (*tshad sog srul ce*) to saw. - 3. *ta śrul-ce* (*rta srul ce*) to put a horse to a gallop?

W. also *śrub* (*srub*), unripe ears of wheat etc. *śrub nyé-ce* (*srub nye ce*) W. to rub them between the hands; the grains, thus being shelled, are considered a rural dainty; ₒbrás-kyi srus ('bras kyi srus) a shelled grain of rice.
Sch. to thicken, to become more consistent, by evaporation, by boiling.
Wdn., sred (sred) S.g., a species of corn (?).
I. sbst. a certain shrub Cs.
